item.title: Masked people attack Ctg ISP over extortion, rob Tk 35 lakh
item.description: A group of masked armed people attacked and vandalised the office of an internet service provider in the Chakbazar area of Chattogram city on Monday, over a demand for nearly Tk 2 crore in extortion money....

item.title: Maldives president conveys condolences over flood, landslide deaths in Bangladesh
item.description: The president of Maldives, Dr Mohamed Muizzu has extended their deepest sympathies and condolences to prime minister Tarique Rahman, the government, and the people of Bangladesh as they confront the severe impacts of the ongoing monsoon floods and landslides....

item.title: 2 arrested for beating elderly man to death at Lalbagh
item.description: The police arrested two individuals on charge of beating an elderly man to death allegedly over payment of house rent in the capital’s Lalbagh area on Monday....News

item.description: A group of masked armed men attacked and vandalised the office of an internet service provider in the Chakbazar area of Chattogram city on Monday, over a demand for nearly Tk 2 crore in extortion.......

item.description: The police arrested two individuals accused of beating an elderly man to death allegedly over payment of house rent in the capital’s Lalbagh area on Monday......

item.title: UK unveils plan to ban Iran Revolutionary Guards, says ministry
item.description: The UK government announced plans on Monday to ban Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a threat to national security, alongside an Iran-linked group accused of a series of attacks against the Jewish community......Arabs& Middle Easterners

item.title: India thrash England in historic first women's Test at Lord's
item.description: Sneh Rana and Deepti Sharma shared the last four wickets as India completed a 270-run thrashing of England to win the first women's Test at Lord's on Monday....

item.title: England open door to Flower return after McCullum axed as Test coach
item.description: England have left the door ajar for Andy Flower to return as their Test coach after announcing Sunday that Brendon McCullum had been sacked from his red-ball role....

item.title: US-Bangla to add 21 Boeing aircraft to its fleet in 2027
item.description: Private carrier US-Bangla Airlines will add 21 brand-new Boeing 737-8 aircraft to its fleet in 2027 through a US$1.11 billion investment, marking the largest fleet expansion ever undertaken by a Bangladeshi airline......

item.title: Sinner: Quiet man basks in the limelight
item.description: Jannik Sinner is the quiet man of tennis who has become a big noise, clinching a second consecutive Wimbledon title and fifth Grand Slam crown....

item.title: Temple manager on 4-day police remand in money laundering case
item.description: A Dhaka metropolitan magistrate court on Monday remanded Gaibandha temple manager in police custody for four days for interrogation in a money laundering case over suspicious financial transactions of Tk 9.35 crore....

item.title: 482 weather incidents hit Rohingya camps, affecting 43,000 dwellers
item.description: At least 482 weather-related incidents were reported in Rohingya camps between July 5 and 12, 2026, affecting around 43,000 people from 9,463 households......City& Local Guides

item.title: Bellingham drags England but Tuchel demands more
item.description: Jude Bellingham has dragged England into the World Cup semi-finals but criticism from manager Thomas Tuchel of his side's quality has sparked a fresh standoff between the pair before Wednesday's showdown against Argentina....

item.title: Iran military warns US against interference in Hormuz
item.description: Iran’s military warned on Monday that it would not allow the United States to ‘interfere’ in the management of the Strait of Hormuz as hostilities with Washington resumed....

item.title: France powers down nuclear reactors over heatwave
item.description: France’s main energy provider on Sunday said that three nuclear reactors had been temporarily shut down and eight others were operating at reduced power due to the heatwave sweeping France......GeographicReference

item.title: 'Final before final': France face Spain in World Cup blockbuster
item.description: Spain will attempt to neutralise one of the most potent attacking arsenals in World Cup history on Tuesday when they take on France in a heavyweight semi-final showdown....

item.title: Oil prices jump above 3.5pc on renewed US-Iran fighting
item.description: Oil prices opened sharply higher on Monday after the United States launched a wave of attacks on Iran and as Tehran announced it would close the Strait of Hormuz......

item.title: One more child dies with measles symptoms, 1,009 infected in 24 hours
item.description: One more child died with measles symptoms across Bangladesh in the past 24 hours, while 1,009 new infections were reported during the same period......InternationalSports Competitions

item.title: DSE benchmark index climbs to year’s highest point
item.description: Dhaka Stock Exchange's benchmark index, DSEX, climbed to its highest point of 2026 on Monday, gaining a combined 62 points over two consecutive sessions of trading......

item.title: Latest US strikes have 'rendered futile' recent diplomacy: Iran
item.description: Iran has condemned the latest wave of US attacks on its territory, saying they had 'rendered futile' all the diplomatic efforts of the last few months......

item.title: Govt opens dedicated control room for flood-time power coordination
item.description: The Power Division has opened a dedicated control room to ensure uninterrupted electricity supply and strengthen coordination among power utilities during the ongoing flood situation in parts of the country......

item.title: Drone attack sparks fire at industrial zone in southern Russia
item.description: A drone attack sparked a fire at an industrial zone in Russia's southern Stavropol, the governor said on Monday, as Ukraine intensifies its strikes in response to Moscow's barrages.......

item.title: US will be ‘paid’ for guarding Strait of Hormuz: Trump
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday that the United States would be paid for guarding the Strait of Hormuz after declaring that it would be ‘taking over’ the strategic waterway....

item.title: More than 2,700 died as result of heatwaves in England: researchers
item.description: At least 2,700 people died in England and Wales as a result of heatwaves that struck in May and June, according to a study released on Monday......Environmentclimate change

item.title: Man sentenced to death for raping madrassah student in Jaipurhat
item.description: The Women and Children Repression Prevention Tribunal in Jaipurhat on Monday sentenced a man to death for raping a madrassah student in 2023.......

item.title: NCP MP questions if BNP seeks one-party rule thru reserved-seats
item.description: National Citizen Party lawmaker Akhter Hossen on Monday questioned whether the BNP was attempting to establish a one-party system by assigning its reserved-seat lawmakers responsibilities in constituencies of opposition lawmakers....Arabs& Middle Easterners

item.title: UK announces £250-million package to protect Jewish community
item.description: Britain's government on Monday announced more than £250 million to boost police protection of the Jewish community, which in recent months has been targeted by a series of attacks......

item.title: Fire rages in Fontainebleau forest near Paris
item.description: French officials rushed two firefighting planes to the Paris region Sunday, after a fire erupted south of the French capital, disrupting traffic during a busy summer travel weekend and piling more misery on a region sweltering through its latest heatwave......

item.title: US interested in collecting, preserving Nazrul’s literary masterpieces: envoy
item.description: Ambassador of the United States to Bangladesh Brent T Christensen has said that the US is highly interested in collecting and preserving the literary masterpieces of national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam....

item.title: US envoy says his country invested over $1m in Bangladesh heritage preservation
item.description: US ambassador to Bangladesh Brent T Christensen on Monday said that the United States has invested more than $1 million through the Ambassador’s Fund for Cultural Preservation to support heritage conservation projects in Bangladesh over the past 25 years.......

item.title: PM heads committee on Rohingya repatriation strategy
item.description: The government has formed an 11-member national committee, headed by the prime minister, to formulate a strategy for the speedy and sustainable repatriation of Rohingyas and coordinate the roles of law enforcement and intelligence agencies involved in the process....

item.title: PM to head national committee on Rohingya repatriation strategy
item.description: The government has formed an 11-member national committee, headed by prime minister Tarique Rahman, to formulate a national strategy for the speedy and sustainable repatriation of Rohingyas and to coordinate the roles of law enforcement and intelligence agencies involved in the process....

item.title: Nearly 33,000 arrested in nationwide anti-crime crackdown
item.description: The police have arrested nearly 33,000 crime suspects in a nationwide special operation launched in around two and a half month, as authorities continue a broad crackdown on crime and illegal drugs......

item.title: Mosque-wise probe into model mosque project irregularities ordered
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Monday asked the religious affairs ministry to conduct mosque-by-mosque investigations into alleged irregularities in the model mosque project......GeographicReference

item.title: Saudi envoy discusses Hajj costs with aviation minister
item.description: Ambassador of the Saudi Arabia to Bangladesh Abdullah ibn Dhafer ibn Ubayya paid a courtesy call on civil aviation and tourism minister Afroza Khanam and state minister M Rashiduzzaman Millat at the Bangladesh Secretariat in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Saudi support sought to reduce Hajj travel costs for Bangladeshis
item.description: Civil aviation and tourism minister Afroza Khanam on Monday sought Saudi Arabia's cooperation in reducing the overall cost of hajj for Bangladeshi pilgrims by lowering taxes and charges on Hajj air travel.......

item.description: A CASE was filed on Sunday night against a teenager allegedly for raping a 10-year-old madrassah student in Kumarkhali upazila of Kushtia......

item.title: Matarbari deep-sea port likely to open in 2029
item.description: The first deep-sea port of Bangladesh at Matarbari in Cox’s Bazar is scheduled to become operational in 2029, shipping minister Sheikh Rabiul Alam told the Jatiya Sangsad on Monday......

item.title: Matarbari deep-sea port to begin operations in 2029: minister
item.description: Bangladesh’s first deep-sea port at Matarbari in Cox’s Bazar is scheduled to become operational in 2029, enabling large mother vessels to berth directly for the first time and significantly reducing the country’s dependence on foreign transshipment hubs, shipping minister Sheikh Rabiul Alam told the Jatiya Sangsad on Monday....Bangladeshpolitics analysis

item.title: 33,000 posts vacant in Bangladesh public health sector
item.description: Nearly 33,000 posts for doctors, nurses and frontline health workers remained vacant across the country’s public healthcare system, health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain told Jatiya Sangsad on Monday......

item.title: Bangladesh declares half-day state mourning for ex-amir of Qatar
item.description: Bangladesh on Monday paid tribute to former amir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, with local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir signing the condolence book at the Qatari embassy in Dhaka and announcing half-day state mourning on July 16.......

item.title: 92pc with mental health conditions get no treatment: minister
item.description: More than 92 per cent of people living with mental health conditions in Bangladesh do not receive any form of treatment, health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain told Jatiya Sangsad on Monday....News

item.title: No plan to cancel or renegotiate Laldia Terminal deal: minister
item.description: The government did not have any plan to cancel or renegotiate the concession agreement signed with Denmark-based APM Terminals for the establishment and operation of the Laldia Container Terminal at the Chattogram Port, shipping minister Sheikh Rabiul Alam told Jatiya Sangsad on Monday....

item.title: No plan to cancel, renegotiate Laldia Terminal deal, minister tells JS

item.description: Investigators combed on Monday through the wreckage of a huge fire at a Bangkok bar that killed least 27 people and injured dozens, Thailand’s worst such incident for almost two decades....

item.description: Gold prices in Bangladesh on Monday dropped after the Bangladesh Jewellers Association announced a Tk 2,216 reduction per bhari for 22-carat gold......

item.title: Gold prices fall again
item.description: The Bangladesh Jewellers Association on Monday cut the price of gold for another time, slashing the rate of 22-carat gold by Tk 2,216 per bhori to set the new price including value added tax at Tk 221,966......

item.description: The rights body in a statement said that the Bangladesh government, and the United Nations and donor governments and agencies should reduce overcrowding in the camps......Environmentclimate change

item.description: ‘Jurassic Park’ star Sam Neill died in Australia on Monday aged 78, his family said in a statement that described the beloved actor’s death as ‘sudden and unexpected’......

item.description: The government on Monday reiterated that former prime minister Sheikh Hasina would have to land in jail if she surrenders, while efforts to extradite her are underway......

item.title: EU to limit children’s access to social media
item.description: EU chief Ursula von der Leyen said on Monday that children should have ‘phased and gradual access’ to social media as experts recommended under 13s only use online platforms under supervision....ExecutiveBranch

item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Monday expressed confidence that the Bangladesh Army will earn greater international recognition, prestige and professional distinction in the years ahead, pledging the government’s full support for enhancing its capabilities and accelerating its modernisation......

item.description: A local leader of Juba Dal was stabbed to death allegedly by his rivals over previous enmity in Bagharpara upazila of Jashore district on Monday......

item.description: A fire in a bar in the Thai capital Bangkok killed at least 27 people late Sunday night, the Thai prime minister, Anutin Charnvirakul, told local media......

item.description: Police recovered the bodies of a man and his wife from their home in Mahadevpur upazila of Naogaon on Monday morning......

item.title: Argentina football great Rattin dies at 89
item.description: Former Argentina captain Antonio Rattin, who was sent off in the 1966 World Cup quarter-final against England, died on Saturday at the age of 89, his club Boca Juniors confirmed....History

item.description: Jannik Sinner was at his clinical best as he successfully defended his Wimbledon title with a bruising four-set victory over French Open champion Alexander Zverev in Sunday's final....

item.title: Turkey arrests rock star, activist Haluk Levent
item.description: Police on Sunday arrested Turkish rock star Haluk Levent, a civil society activist whose NGO rose to prominence for its aid work following the 2023 earthquake, media reports said......
